entry.title: Re: Environmental Details tab missing in Manage Locations...
entry.content:
Hello,

there are a few settings/configuration I would like you to check to have the "Environmental Details" be visible.

  1. In the IMG configuration, check the so-called "Location Roles". This is a set of roles which must be set up first. Please have a look at SPRO: Environment, Health and Safety -> Environment Management -> Master Data Configuration -> Locations -> Specify Roles of Persons Responsible for Locations.  The technical role name/code has to match a PFCG role, as an example, you can use the name SAP_EHSM_HSS_ENVMGR.
  2. Set up the PFCG role. The technical role name/code used in the previous step has to be a PFCG role. It does not need to hold any authorizations or menu entries, but it is only required to flag certain users.
  3. In the IMG configuration, check the so-called "Specify Activities for Persons Responsible in Environment Management". This is an additional setting which must be made for the role created in step 1. Please have a look at SPRO: Environment, Health and Safety -> Environment Management -> General Configuration -> Specify Activities for Persons Responsible in Environment Management.  Following the example, your role SAP_EHSM_HSS_ENVMGR must be entered here and the checkbox for "Manage" must be set.
  4. Assign the PFCG role to your users.

As a summary, you have to have a PFCG-Role and an Person Responsible Role which are named the same. Then, the person responsible role has to be set as responsible in the Environment Management configuration. The role SAP_BR_ENVIRONMENTAL_MANAGER is a so-called Frontend-Role which only holds the information for the SAP Fiori Launchpad (Tiles, Catalogs and Spaces).

With kind regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Ability to Ship Quality Stock from Plant to Plant
entry.content:
From a compliance and audit perspective, SAP strongly discourages bypassing the inspection process. The safest and most supportable method is:

  1. Perform UD at the 3PL plant.

  2. Move stock to unrestricted.

  3. Ship via STO to the receiving plant.

If your business case demands direct Q‑Stock transfer, it should be handled via custom development with clear governance.

entry.title: Re: Table A016 2 Valid Pricing Conditions
entry.content: To answer your first question, no it is not possible to maintain two condition records with the same validity period.  To answer your second one, system will not allow you to maintain condition record with blank Valid-From field.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM の「設定およびセキュリティ分析」で何ができるのか
entry.content:
本ブログは、2025年12月に実施した以下のセッションを紹介する記事となります。
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XRT_ZwH_ELw&list=PLMaiefj1fjrE&index=4&t=13s
Configuration & Security Analysis の最新機能については、参考リンクにある情報を参照してください。

運用しているすべてのSAPシステム／クラウドサービスが、監査基準に準拠し、適切なセキュリティ設定で運用されているか。この問いに答えるための SAP Cloud ALM 標準機能「設定およびセキュリティ分析（Configuration & Security Analysis / CSA）」について、コンセプト・機能詳細・今後のロードマップを整理して紹介します。

本記事の内容
  1. 記事の目的と対象読者
  2. 設定およびセキュリティ分析（CSA）とは
  3. 仕組み ― データ収集・蓄積・診断／分析
  4. 設定方法と2つのタイル
  5. 機能詳細① ABAP Platform 向け
  6. 機能詳細② Cloud Service（BTP）向け
  7. 機能詳細③ 設定値の検証（チェック）
  8. 今後のロードマップ
  9. まとめ

1 記事の目的と対象読者

SAP Cloud ALM の「設定およびセキュリティ分析（英語名：Configuration & Security Analysis、略してCSA）」は、SAP Cloud ALM がリリースされた当初から存在する機能です。これまで大きく取り上げられることの少ない、いわば「地味な」機能でしたが、近年、国内外を問わずセキュリティに対する意識が急速に高まっていることを背景に、来年以降も新機能のリリースが続々と予定されています。

グローバルの機能オーナーも「眠れる森の美女が動き出す」と表現するほど、今まさに進化のフェーズに入った機能です。本記事では、この CSA で 現在何ができるのか、そして 今後何ができるようになるのか を整理して紹介します。

対象読者

SAPシステムのセキュリティ・運用を担う Basis／システム管理担当者、および セキュリティ担当者（CISO配下を含む）。SAP Cloud ALM を利用してシステムのセキュリティ設定を可視化・点検したいと考えている方を想定しています。

2 設定およびセキュリティ分析（CSA）とは

企業のセキュリティ担当者やシステム管理者は、自身が運用するすべてのシステム／サービスが「監査基準に準拠し、適切なセキュリティ体制で運用されていること」を確実にする必要があります。SAP システムでいえば、SAP のセキュリティ推奨事項や企業独自のセキュリティ対策が適切に適用されているかを継続的に管理・改善していかなければなりません。

こうしたニーズに応えるために提供されているのが CSA です。CSA は、管理対象システムの設定データを SAP Cloud ALM の中央データベース CCDB（Configuration Change Database） に収集し、セキュアかつ Read-only なアクセスのもとで、設定データや変更内容の 手動および自動での分析 を可能にします。

図1. CSA のコンセプト ― ビジネスニーズ・ビジョン・提供価値

提供価値（What）とビジョン

  • 設定データの中央収集：技術設定を CCDB に集約し、対象システムにログインせずとも参照・分析できる。
  • 設定値のバリデーション（新機能）：セキュリティベースラインへの準拠状況を自動で評価する機能が新たに提供された。
  • ビジョン：技術担当者でも業務担当者でも、AI 支援による自動の As-is／To-be 分析を、ニーズに合わせて簡単に実施できるようにする。すぐに始められ、結果を扱いやすくすることも継続的に改善していく。

現行機能と計画されている機能


図2. CSA の機能概要 ― オンプレミス／クラウド双方の設定を CCDB に集約

現行機能 計画されている機能

技術設定などを定期収集し、SAP Cloud ALM 上（CCDB）に格納To-Be と As-Is の状況を比較する設定の検証
収集した情報をストアブラウザから照会重大な問題を通知するための組込型アラート
選択したスコープ／時間枠における設定の変更分析組込型分析による、問題の傾向および根本原因の分析
設定アイテムをパターンベースで探せる検索機能―

3 仕組み ― データ収集・蓄積・診断／分析

CSA は大きく 「データ収集」→「データ蓄積」→「診断・分析」 の3ステップで動作します。

 


図3. データ収集・蓄積・診断／分析の全体像

データ収集は管理対象システムへエージェントレスで接続できるため、SAP Solution Manager のように診断エージェントをインストールする必要はありません。一度接続してしまえば、設定データは CCDB に自動収集・格納され、定期収集によって変更履歴も蓄積されていきます。CCDB に集約されたデータを使って分析を行うため、管理対象システムへ変更や影響を与えることなく、ログインもせずに設定データを確認できます。

4 設定方法と2つのタイル

接続設定はとてもシンプル


図4. 接続設定 ― Cloud Services は「データ収集を ON にするだけ」
  • Cloud Services：自動的に SAP Cloud ALM のランドスケープ管理に登録されるため、対象サービスのデータ収集を ON にするだけで完了。初期状態はすべて OFF のため、ON にし忘れないよう注意。
    ※初回は全データ収集に24時間ほどかかる場合があります。
  • ABAP Platform：トランザクション /sdf/alm_setup でデータ収集設定が完了。データは ST-A/PI コンポーネントを使って収集。この設定はヘルス監視や統合監視など他のオペレーション機能と共通です。

分析に使う2つのタイル

CCDB に収集したデータを分析する際は、次の2つのタイルを使い分けます。


図5. CSA の2つのタイル ―「データストア」と「チェック（New）」

タイル できること

データストアストアブラウザ（設定データの参照）／変更（変更分析）／検索（パターンベース検索）
チェック New設定値のバリデーションを実施し、コンプライアンスの点数や評価を算出

5 機能詳細① ABAP Platform

ストアブラウザと設定ストア（Config Store）

CCDB の中では、設定データは 設定ストア（Config Store） と呼ばれる単位で格納されています。設定ストアは「同じセマンティック（文脈）を持つ設定データをまとめた箱」とイメージしてください。たとえば SAP_KERNEL という設定ストアには、カーネルのバージョン情報や利用 OS 情報などが紐づきます。


図6. ストアブラウザ ― 設定ストアを選び、その中の設定値を参照する

ABAP システムで確認できる設定ストアには、次のようなものがあります。

  • 適用済み SAP Note の一覧
  • インスタンスレベルのパラメータ設定（プロファイルパラメータ）
  • 有効化されている HTTP サービスの一覧
  • 設定変更やクライアント変更の履歴（例：SE06 のグローバル制御）
  • インストールされているソフトウェアコンポーネントやバージョン情報
  • ロックされているトランザクションコードの一覧

図7. ABAP タイプの管理対象システムで利用可能な設定ストアの例
活用例：重要セキュリティ Note の適用状況を確認する
たとえば CVSS 9.9 と極めて深刻度の高い NW ABAP のセキュリティ Note（例：3537476）について「本当に対応済みか」を CISO から問われたとします。適用済み SAP Note の設定ストアを Note 番号で検索すれば、そのシステムに当該 Note が適用されているかどうかを、システムにログインすることなく即座に確認できます。

変更ビュー（変更分析）

設定ストアに格納されたシステム設定値の 変更履歴を一覧で確認 できます。特定日付の設定ストアをドリルダウンすると、その日に変更された設定が一覧表示され、CSV ダウンロードも可能です。


図8. 変更ビュー ― 指定日以降に変更された設定を一覧で確認
活用例：「ある日から挙動が変わった」を追跡する
エンドユーザから「11月17日以降、使っているアプリケーションの挙動が変わった」と問い合わせがあった場合、その日付をドリルダウンすると、当日に実施された移送や権限系パラメータの変更を特定できます。移送番号から責任者を辿り、挙動変化との関連を切り分けていく、といった原因調査に活用できます。

6 機能詳細② Cloud Service（BTP）

クラウドサービスも「設定ストア」という単位で収集データを確認できる点は ABAP と同じですが、大きな違いは コンプライアンス判定が付与される ことです。

クラウドサービスの設定ストアには、SAP Help Portal で公開されている SAP BTP Security Recommendations に基づいたコンプライアンス判定が付与されます。判定はデータを SAP Cloud ALM に送信する前に行われ、実際の設定データに次の2つのカラムが追加されます。

カラム 意味

SECREC_INDEXセキュリティ推奨事項のインデックス ID（例：BTP-IAS-003）
SECREC_STATUSCOMPLIANT または NONCOMPLIANT

図9. BTP 向けの設定ストア ― セキュリティ推奨への準拠状況が付与される

活用例：IAS の Critical なセキュリティ設定を確認する

SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication（IAS）では、セキュリティ強化の観点で次のような推奨事項があります。

  • 管理コンソールへのアクセスに多要素認証（MFA）を必須化する
  • 可能な場合はパスワードレス認証（証明書や FIDO など）を利用する
  • セルフ登録およびソーシャルサインオンを無効にする
  • 既存のコーポレート IDP と連携する／サードパーティ IDP との信頼関係を管理する

図10. IAS におけるセキュリティ強化の推奨事項（例）

これらの推奨事項には BTP-IAS-003 のようなインデックス ID が定義されています。この セキュリティインデックス ID で設定ストア内を検索 することで、現在の設定がコンプライアンスに準拠しているか（COMPLIANT か NONCOMPLIANT か）を確認できます。


図11. セキュリティインデックス ID で検索し、Critical な推奨の準拠を確認
検索では見つからない＝「そもそも未設定」というケースも
実際に検索してみると、あるインデックス（例：管理ユーザの MFA に関する BTP-IAS-001）が「データ無し」となることがあります。これは設定値がそもそも入っていない可能性を示唆します。この見落としがちなケースは、次章の「チェック（設定値の検証）」機能で明確に検知できます。

7 機能詳細③ New 設定値の検証（チェック）

今年新たにリリースされたのが「チェック」タイルによる 設定値の検証（バリデーション） 機能です。ABAP Platform・Cloud Service のそれぞれについて、セキュリティベースラインに基づくコンプライアンスチェックを自動で実施し、準拠率（％）と評価を提示します。ドリルダウンすれば、どの項目が準拠・未準拠だったのかまで確認できます。


図12. チェックタイル ― システムごとのコンプライアンス準拠率と評価を一覧表示

チェックされる項目の例

対象 チェック内容の例

ABAP PlatformSE06 の Global Change Option が修正可能状態になっていないか／SAP* などの高権限ユーザーが無効化されているか（EWA で確認される重要なセキュリティ設定を自動チェック）
Cloud ServiceSAP BTP Security Recommendations のうち、優先度が Critical なものに準拠しているか

図13. セキュリティベースラインに基づく検証項目（今後も拡張予定）
「設定値がそもそも無い」を赤信号で検知
前章の検索では見つからなかった IAS の MFA 設定（BTP-IAS-001）も、チェック機能では 赤信号（未準拠） として明確に表示されます。「設定値がそもそも入っていない」という状態を検知できるのが、検証機能の大きな価値です。

8 今後のロードマップ

CSA は今まさに機能拡張が加速している領域です。2025〜2026年にかけて、次のような機能強化が計画されています。


図14. 2025〜2026 にかけての機能計画

特にホーム画面の刷新では、コンプライアンス度合いや Red / Yellow / Green のメトリクス、最も古い Red メトリクスの経過日数などを一目で把握できるよう改善が進められています（Lab Preview）。


図15. ホーム画面の拡充イメージ（Lab Preview）― KPI とメトリクスの可視化

Solution Manager ConfigVal からの移行と CEI

SAP は、2026年末までに SAP Solution Manager の ConfigVal から SAP Cloud ALM の CSA へ移行できるよう、お客様の優先事項に沿った製品準備を進めています。この方向性は Customer Engagement Initiative（CEI） を通じてお客様の声を反映しながら策定されています。


図16. ConfigVal → CSA 移行のロードマップと CEI プロジェクト
Solution Manager ConfigVal をお使いの方へ
現在 Solution Manager の ConfigVal 機能を利用されているお客様は、CEI プロジェクトを通じて移行のインプットを提供いただけます。関心のある方は SAP 担当者までお問い合わせください。

9 まとめ

本記事では、SAP Cloud ALM の「設定およびセキュリティ分析（CSA）」について、コンセプトから機能詳細、今後のロードマップまでを紹介しました。ポイントを整理すると次のとおりです。

  • CSA は、ABAP Platform / Cloud Service の設定データを エージェントレスで CCDB に集約し、システムにログインせずに設定の参照・変更分析・検索ができる。
  • クラウドサービスでは SAP BTP Security Recommendations への準拠状況がコンプライアンス判定として付与される。
  • 新機能の 「チェック（設定値の検証）」により、セキュリティベースラインへの準拠を自動評価し、「未設定」まで赤信号で検知できる。
  • 2025〜2026年にかけて、検証項目の拡充・アラート／通知・ホーム画面刷新など、機能拡張が加速。Solution Manager ConfigVal からの移行先としても整備が進む。

これまで「地味な機能」だった CSA は、セキュリティ意識の高まりとともに、SAP ランドスケープ全体のセキュリティ設定を可視化・点検する中核機能へと進化しつつあります。まずはお使いの SAP Cloud ALM で、データ収集を ON にして設定を眺めてみることをおすすめします。

 
参考リンク
    • Configuration & Security Analysis | Expert Portal

本記事の内容についてのコメントや、実プロジェクトでのフィードバックをお寄せいただけると幸いです。SAP Cloud ALM に関する情報収集のため、コミュニティのフォローもぜひよろしくお願いいたします。

※ 本記事は SAP 社内セッション資料をもとに再構成したものです。掲載している画面イメージ・ロードマップ（Lab Preview を含む）は、将来予告なく変更される可能性があり、特定の機能・提供時期を約束するものではありません。最新の情報は SAP Help Portal 等の公式情報をご確認ください。

entry.title: Optional permitted values in custom tags are not working anymore
entry.content: In der Cloud Integration sind unter Custom Tags neuerdings keine Optionalen Werte mehr möglich. Die aktuelle Version verlang zwingend einen Wert in dem Feld "Permitted Values". Somit sind keine Freitexte mehr für die Entwickler möglich.

entry.title: Re: ZICM Condition Type Missing in PO
entry.content:
The fact that ZICM appears in manual PO but not in PR‑based PO strongly indicates a schema determination mismatch. The PR is driving a different pricing schema or requirement logic, which excludes ZICM.

My suggestion is to run a condition analysis (ME21N → Conditions → Analysis) for both cases (manual PO vs PR‑based PO). That will show you exactly why ZICM is skipped.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation flow failing to run

Hello SAP,
We are currently in the process of configuring the flexible workflow for purchase requisitions. However, when attempting to create a purchase requisition using the APP ID F2229, we encounter the following error message: "There are no steps available. Neither existing steps nor a matching preview can be determined." This error appears on the screen prior to saving the requisition.

For more information, please check the attached document.

Regards, Rajesh Sharma

entry.title: Teaching Students to Work With an Unreliable but Inevitable Teammate: AI
entry.content:
I had the opportunity to present a teaching seminar at SAP ACC APAC 2026 today on a challenge my colleagues and I have been noticing across our classes over the last couple of years.

AI is already in the room.

Whether we account for it in our subject design or not, students are already using it.

What started to catch our attention was a shift. In some cases, weaker students were producing stronger coursework but performing worse in exams. Stronger students remained capable, but often became more inconsistent. Class discussions became quieter. Students increasingly arrived with answers, but less of the thinking behind those answers.

The more we talked about it as a teaching team, the more we realised the issue wasn't that students were using AI.

It was that many had never been taught how to work with AI.

Instead, AI often became a one-shot answer machine. Ask a question, accept the answer, move on.

Rather than trying to remove AI from the learning process, we took a different approach. We redesigned parts of our subjects around the assumption that AI was already present.

Content moved out of class.

Thinking moved into class.

We started spending more class time questioning, challenging, refining and discussing answers, including answers generated by AI. The goal wasn't to make AI disappear. The goal was to make the thinking visible again.

One idea that seemed to resonate during the session was thinking of AI as a teammate.

A teammate who is fast, always available and often provides a reasonable answer.

But also a teammate who is not always right and rarely provides the full story.

The value isn't necessarily in getting an answer. The value is in the interaction. Asking follow-up questions. Checking assumptions. Challenging conclusions. Refining ideas.

For me, one of the biggest changes has been how I think about class time.

Class isn't where answers happen.

Class is where answers get challenged.

And perhaps that's the bigger shift. The goal isn't better answers. The goal is better thinking about answers.

Ironically, after the session I drafted a forum post reflecting on these ideas, and the platform blocked it because it detected AI-assisted content.

So, in what felt like the most appropriate response possible given the topic of the presentation, I asked AI to make it sound more like me and a bit more human.

I'm still not entirely sure whether that's a success story or a cautionary tale.

Either way, the irony wasn't lost on me. 😄

I'd be interested to hear what others are seeing in their own teaching.

How are you helping students move beyond using AI as an answer generator and towards using it as a thinking partner?

 

Yes written with AI(copilot).

entry.title: Re: encounter the following error message: There are no s...
entry.content:
The error does not mean your PR is wrong. It means no active workflow definition matches your PR data. Flexible workflow always checks for a valid definition before saving, so if none exists, you get this message.

My suggestion is go into the Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions app, create a simple test workflow (e.g., “All PRs above 1 INR → Approver = yourself”), activate it, and then retry in F2229. That will confirm the setup is working.

entry.title: Re: Why does data extraction annotation fail only on tabl...
entry.content:
@roland_szajko 

Hi Roland,

Sorry for the late reply.

The error occurs when activating the CDS view.

I tried the following 3 patterns, all of which failed at activation:

[Pattern 1] select from SE11 database view with CDC mapping to physical table PRHI

@Analytics: {
dataExtraction: {
enabled: true,
delta.changeDataCapture: {
mapping:[ {
table : 'PRHI', role: #MAIN,
viewElement : ['posnr'],
tableElement : ['posnr']
}]
}
}
}
define view ZCDS_TEST as select from z_base_v
{
key posnr,
psphi,
z_up,
z_down,
z_left,
z_right
}

Background: z_base_v is an SE11 database view that renames reserved keyword
fields (LEFT, RIGHT) from PRHI to Z_LEFT, Z_RIGHT.

[Pattern 2] select from PRHI directly with table alias

@Analytics: {
dataExtraction: {
enabled: true,
delta.changeDataCapture: {
mapping:[ {
table : 'PRHI', role: #MAIN,
viewElement : ['posnr'],
tableElement : ['posnr']
}]
}
}
}
define view ZCDS_TEST as select from prhi as A
{
key A.posnr,
A.psphi,
A.up as z_up,
A.down as z_down,
A.left as z_left, <- Unexpected keyword "left" error
A.right as z_right
}

[Pattern 3] select from PRHI with single quotes around reserved keywords

We also tried wrapping with double quotes, but ADT automatically removes
the double quotes upon saving, resulting in an error.

@Analytics: {
dataExtraction: {
enabled: true,
delta.changeDataCapture: {
mapping:[ {
table : 'PRHI', role: #MAIN,
viewElement : ['posnr'],
tableElement : ['posnr']
}]
}
}
}
define view ZCDS_TEST as select from prhi as A
{
key A.posnr,
A.psphi,
A.up as z_up,
A.down as z_down,
A.'left' as z_left, <- Unexpected word "'left'" error
A.'right' as z_right
}

The root issue seems to be that PRHI has fields named LEFT and RIGHT
which are reserved keywords in CDS, making it impossible to reference
them directly in a CDS view.

Do you have any suggestions on how to handle this?

Thank you.

entry.title: Angklung and Agentic AI?
entry.content:
Hope you all enjoyed the SAP ACC APAC 2026 gala dinner on Monday July 13th! I certainly did. The highlight of the evening was the angklung orchestra where we created lovely music together.

What was magical about it?

  • With the excellent direction of the fabulous conductor, we were all able to create music!
  • At the start of the event, these was just noise, not music.
  • But as the conductor orchestrated the various notes (numbered angklungs), we learnt to follow her instructions.
  • The conductor set the tempo, volume, start and end points.
  • She also pointed out off note players 😁
  • At the end of the session, many diners took home entire sets of angklung to build their own complete melodies. They were so excited to learn their new skills.

What does this have to do with Agentic AI and SAP Joule?

  • The musical instrument Angklung is the tool.
  • The delegates playing the angklung were agents.
  • They (agent swarm) learned the skill play the instrument (by shaking it).
  • They listened/observed for the cue to play their notes (trigger).
  • Most importantly, the conductor (human in the loop) directed the entire process. Set the expectations, called out errors, critiqued and refined the performance.
  • By the end of the evening, we had played three songs - do re mi, can't help falling in love, tiktok!!
  • Without the human director, there would be just cacophony, not music.

Now you see how humans will prompt, direct, guide and correct agents!

In my opinion, this will be the new skill we need to teach our students to prepare them for the autonomous enterprise. SAP Joule will help us get there.

...to be managers of swarms of agents, to evaluate the outcomes, and correct course to achieve desired results...

What are your thoughts?

#sapaccapac2026

entry.title: Successor functions and display-only authorization for CJ01–03/CN21–23 in SAP S/4HANA 2025
entry.content:
We are currently upgrading our system from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 to SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01.

We checked transactions CJ01, CJ02, CJ03, CN21, CN22, and CN23 using SU25 Step 2d.

CJ20N, Project Builder, was displayed as the successor transaction. However, none of the following indicators were set for the old transactions.

Does not exist

Locked

Obsolete

SU22 Status Old Transaction

Based on this result, we understand that these transactions remain available and are not currently classified as obsolete, although migration to CJ20N may be recommended.

Previously, authorization for create, change, and display operations was separated by transaction code as follows.

CJ01 and CN21: Create

CJ02 and CN22: Change

CJ03 and CN23: Display

If these transactions are consolidated into CJ20N, we understand that users must first be authorized to start CJ20N through S_TCODE. Create, change, and display operations within CJ20N would then be controlled through authorization objects.

In a previous internal test, we confirmed that a user could use CJ20N for display purposes only with the following authorization settings.

S_TCODE: CJ20N

C_PROJ_TCD: TRTYP = A

C_AFKO_ACT: PS_ACTVY = 03

Could you please clarify the following questions?

Are CJ01, CJ02, CJ03, CN21, CN22, and CN23 still supported and available in SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01?
Is it correct to understand that these transactions are not currently scheduled for removal, but that migration to CJ20N is recommended?
When migrating a display-only user who previously used CJ03 or CN23 to CJ20N, is the SAP standard authorization concept to grant start authorization for CJ20N through S_TCODE and restrict operations to display through authorization objects such as C_PROJ_TCD and C_AFKO_ACT?
When a user with display-only authorization starts CJ20N, does CJ20N automatically operate in display mode?

Alternatively, can the user select create or change operations on the screen, with authorization checks preventing execution or saving?

Is it correct that there is no single native SAP Fiori application that provides the same complete functional scope as CJ20N?
In SAP S/4HANA 2025, what is SAP's recommended solution for the integrated creation, change, and display of Project Definitions, WBS Elements, Networks, Activities, and Material Components?

Should customers continue using CJ20N, or should they use a combination of native SAP Fiori applications?

We would also appreciate references to any relevant SAP Notes, KBAs, or official documentation.

entry.title: Re: J3RFLVMOBVEDH-Stock Overview
entry.content:
The fact that the columns appear but values are blank means the report is executing correctly but no matching data is being retrieved. This is almost always due to:

  • Wrong/missing input parameters,
  • No stock data for the selection, or
  • Missing localization customizing.

My recommendation is first confirm stock exists with MB52 for the same selection. If MB52 shows values but J3RFLVMOBVEDH does not, then it is a localization customizing issue.

entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, free‑of‑charge deliveries (scope item BDA, order types CBFD/CBXN) always post PGI with movement type 601, hitting the standard COGS account. There is no SSCUI to directly assign a different G/L account for this scenario. The SAP‑recommended approach is to use valuation classes and automatic account determination (GBB/VAX) or split valuation to redirect postings to a “Sample/Marketing Expense” account instead of COGS.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud 2608 / Transfer of Custom Field into...
entry.content:
Hi Lukas,

Short answer: Enabling all business scenarios is the cause, not the fix. For a plain custom field like yours, enabling the one business scenario that matches where the value sits in the sales order is enough — the value is then copied automatically at billing creation. No copy control, routine, or custom logic is needed. Right now, several active scenarios overwrite each other, which is why the invoice stays empty.

Why it shows in the Billing Due List but not on the invoice

The due‑list item references the source sales order, so your field appears there as source data. The billing document is a separate object, populated only by the business‑scenario data transfer at creation time — so seeing it in the due list doesn't mean the transfer to the invoice is active.

Root cause: multiple scenarios overwrite each other

Likely — this matches your setup. When several transfer scenarios are active, the system runs them in a fixed order and the last one wins. SAP Note 2955708 documents exactly this: with both "Sales Document to Billing Document on Header Level" and "Sales Document to Billing Document from Item to Header Level" enabled, the item‑to‑header transfer runs second and overwrites the header value with the (empty) sales‑item value. Reference: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2955708

Note that item → header is fully supported — that is exactly what "Sales Document to Billing Document from Item to Header Level" is for. The point is not that it's impossible, but that only the scenario matching where your value actually sits should be active, so nothing overwrites it with a blank value.

What to do

Keep active only the single scenario that matches your source, and turn the others off:

If the value sits on the sales order header: enable only Sales Document to Billing Document on Header Level and disable the Header‑to‑item scenario (or vice versa).

If the value sits on the sales order item: enable only the item‑to‑header (or item‑to‑item) scenario, and make sure the item field is actually filled.

This is the clean, standard path. I tested it: when you enable the header‑to‑header scenario on the Sales Document field, the system automatically creates the matching field in the Billing Document context — confirming the propagation is wired for you with no further configuration (besides UI adaptation). Once a billing document is created from the order, the value is carried over. Publish the field and test with a new order → new invoice (existing documents are not retro‑filled). For a straight copy of the same field, this standard scenario logic is sufficient — the copy‑control routine setup is only needed for derived/logic‑based transfers, which isn't your case.

Verify:

  • in the field's Business Scenarios tab, only the one matching scenario is ticked.
  • the field exists in the correct target context (Billing Document header vs item) for the level you expect.
  • re‑run with a brand‑new document and a non‑empty source field.

Summary

Turn off the extra scenarios and keep only the one matching where your value sits, publish, and test with a fresh order→invoice. Enabling the header‑to‑header scenario auto‑creates the billing‑context field, so the value flows through with no copy control or custom logic. Activating everything is what triggers the empty overwrite (SAP Note 2955708).

Hope it helps.
Best regards,
Michal

entry.title: Password Reset Workflow for HANA DB Connectors with Global Password Provisioning Disabled
entry.content:
We have an SAP GRC Access Control landscape with the following configuration:

• All SAP ABAP application connectors use SNC authentication.
• Password provisioning is globally disabled in the Global Provisioning Settings.
• SAP HANA DB connectors use User ID and Password authentication (SNC is not enabled).

Business Requirement:

We would like to create a dedicated Access Request Type/MSMP workflow to perform password reset requests only for SAP HANA DB connectors without impacting the existing SNC-enabled provisioning process for ABAP systems.

Our objective is to keep the current global configuration unchanged while allowing password reset functionality only for HANA DB connectors.

We would like SAP's guidance on the following:

1. Is this scenario supported in standard SAP GRC?
2. Can password reset be enabled only for selected HANA DB connectors while the global "Deactivate Password" provisioning setting remains enabled?
3. Is the "Deactivate Password" setting evaluated globally, or can it be controlled based on connector, request type, or provisioning action?
4. Is there any SAP Note or recommended configuration for landscap

entry.title: Re: INTERCOMPANY AND INTRACOMPANY
entry.content:
Dear @AnonymouslyM,

The price difference is standard in Public Cloud. In Advanced Intercompany Sales the purchase order and intercompany sales order prices are determined independently. There is no standard configuration or copying-control setting to copy the PO price automatically into the intercompany sales order. SAP KBA 3742784 confirms that a custom extension for example on SAP BTP is required when automatic transfer is mandatory

More details below:

SAP KBA 3742784 – How to Copy Price From Purchase Order to Intercompany Sales Order

SAP Help – Setting Up Advanced Intercompany Sales

The standard approach is to maintain the required prices separately:

  • Purchase order: maintain purchasing price conditions, for example in the purchasing info record
  • Intercompany sales order: maintain the sales price condition records and the relevant sales pricing procedure

To keep the values equal, maintain the same price, currency, price unit and validity dates on both sides. For an intracompany stock transfer within the same company code an intercompany sales order is normally not created so PO-to-SO price copying is not applicable

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Full import API
entry.content:
Hello @Ritumbhara,

As far as I know, password validation against the current site's password policy is not performed for passwords imported via the Full Import API. During authentication, CDC validates the provided password against the stored password hash, while password policy enforcement is typically applied during standard password flows, such as account registration, password change, or password reset.

At the moment, there is no built-in mechanism to reject a login solely because an imported password does not comply with the current password policy.

Depending on your requirements, you could consider the following options:

  • Force a password change by setting requirePasswordChange to true via accounts.setAccountInfo. This will require the user to change their password on the next successful login, and the new password must comply with the current password policy. However, since your goal is to migrate existing passwords and allow users to continue signing in with them, this option may not fit your use case.
  • Deactivate the account by setting isActive to false via accounts.setAccountInfo. In this case, the user will not be able to authenticate until the account is reactivated. This is only suitable if preventing login is the desired behavior.

So, if the requirement is to allow login only when the imported password already complies with the current password policy, I don't believe CDC provides a direct mechanism to enforce that.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Best practice for...
entry.content: Good day @6-Mar

entry.title: Re: Difference between SAC Enterprise and SAC Embedded (K...
entry.content:
Hello @vlafosse ,

Two different things are getting mixed up here, so let me split it out.

1. SACEE (embedded edition) limitations, and a bigger update: it's deprecated

Worth leading with this: SACEE itself was removed from the SAP Discovery Center service catalog as of March 15, 2025, and is no longer available for new deployments or renewal. If you're on an active CPEA agreement that started before that date, existing instances keep working until that contract expires, but that's it, no new tenants, no renewals.

For context, here were SACEE's limitations vs Enterprise SAC while it was still available:

  1. Live connectivity only, and only to HANA Cloud on BTP/Cloud Foundry. No import, no other sources.
  2. No Planning, no Predictive/Smart Predict, no Analytics Designer. Stories/BI only, including no predefined Analytic Applications.
  3. Max 150 concurrent user sessions per tenant.
  4. No connection or admin config via the UI, only the Admin API, and that API can create/delete connections but not edit them.
  5. Cloud Foundry only, not Neo.

SAP's direction now is to point everyone toward full-use SAC public edition instead of a separate limited embedded tenant, with tighter native integration into things like S/4HANA Cloud for dashboards and planning.

2. Publish to Catalog and S/4HANA

Separately, this looks like a mix-up between two similarly named but unrelated features. Publish to Catalog just shares a story with other users/teams inside the same SAC tenant, via the Analytics Catalog. It doesn't push anything into S/4HANA, and it has nothing to do with S/4HANA's own native "Embedded Analytics", that's a different product despite the name overlap.

So no, it doesn't enable S/4HANA consumption, and no extra fee either, it's just governed by your normal Enterprise SAC license and roles (Publish Content permission, or Admin/BI Admin).

One more licensing note: separately from SACEE's deprecation above, SAP Analytics Cloud as a whole (public edition) was also removed from the list of Eligible Cloud Services for new BTPEA/CPEA/PAYG subscriptions as of Jan 1, 2026. Existing contracts keep working, but new SAC purchases now go through SAP Business Data Cloud instead of standalone BTP. Two separate deprecation events, worth not conflating them.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Minor difference * INR 10 cannot be allocated across company codes
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have posted a Down Payment of INR 1,200 in Company Code 1001. We now want to clear/pay this advance from our Parent Company Code 1000 in USD currency, where the equivalent amount is USD 13.72.

However, during the payment process, although we are paying USD 13.72, the actual advance amount being cleared is INR 1,200. When we simulate the payment transaction, the system throws the following error:

"Minor difference INR 10 cannot be allocated across company codes."

entry.title: Re: Regarding ATP verification based on variant selection
entry.content:
Dear @shiwa,

Advanced Variant Configuration and ATP can be used in Public Cloud. The fact that the system is a TDD environment does not change the functional behavior, although the required scope and entitlements must be activated in that environment

However the requested process is not available as a standard dynamic, multilevel ATP check during characteristic selection. When the configurable product is entered in a sales order, Advanced Variant Configuration determines the valid configuration and can select the relevant BOM components based on the characteristic values. The sales-order Product Availability Check is nevertheless performed for the sales-order product, plant, quantity and material availability date

Depending on the configured checking rule, ATP can consider stock and selected supply elements, including purchase requisitions, purchase orders, planned orders and production orders. However, these are supply elements for the product being checked. Standard ATP does not dynamically explode the configuration-specific BOM, perform ATP for every selected component and derive the finished-product delivery date from the component with the latest availability date

For make-to-order production with Variant Configuration the sales-order configuration is transferred to planning and production. MRP can determine the required configuration-specific components, and component availability can subsequently be checked for the planned or production order. This check occurs in production planning and does not provide an immediate component-based delivery-date confirmation while the user is selecting characteristics in the sales order

A possible standard workaround is Variant Matching. For frequently used configurations, separate material variants can be created and stocked or planned individually. When the selected characteristic values fully match a material variant, the material variant can replace the configurable product in the sales-order item. ATP is then performed for that specific material variant and can consider its stock and receipt elements. This approach is practical only when the relevant configuration combinations can be represented by a manageable number of material variants

So standard ATP can confirm the configurable finished product or a matched material variant. It cannot calculate the delivery date dynamically by checking all configuration-specific components selected through the characteristic values

More details below:

SAP Help Advanced Variant Configuration

SAP Help Defining the Product Availability Check

SAP Help Availability Checks

SAP Help Product Availability Check

SAP Help Checking Rules for the Product Availability Check

SAP Help Strategies for Configurable Materials

SAP Help How to Check Component Availability

SAP Help Variant Matching

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Simplified and Flexible SAP Concur Integration for Service Order Expense Posting
entry.content:
Hi,

Great overview!

Just a quick note: in most cases, no manual steps are needed to replicate master data objects (such as service order items) from S/4HANA to Concur, or to import transactional data (such as expense reports) from Concur into S/4HANA. Once master data replication is set up, a one-time manual replication is required to ensure that all relevant master data objects are transferred to Concur. After that, any newly created or updated master data objects will be picked up by scheduled jobs and synchronized with Concur automatically. Similarly, the feedback loop described in step 5 is typically handled by an automated job. Depending on how the job is scheduled, expense reports can be retrieved from Concur as frequently as every 10 minutes.

Best Regards, Andreas

entry.title: Re: Asset not fully depreciating with Dep to day approach...
entry.content:
Kindly verify whether any scrap value, memo value, or cutoff value has been defined for the asset, as these parameters influence depreciation calculations and may result in a residual amount remaining on the asset after the end of its useful life.

For further details regarding scrap value, memo value, and cutoff value please refer to the following KBA:

3620966 - Differences between scrap value, memo value and cutoff value

For additional information on the "depreciation to the day" functionality, kindly refer to the following documentations:

1940343 - Depreciations to the day

3423823 - Depreciation to the day is set in the depreciation key - The calculation of the remaining useful life from the useful life is not clear

3501221 - Remaining useful life calculation for daily depreciation key (and 24 posting periods)

entry.title: FI Documents Missing an ODN? The ODN Insert Report Fixes That in One Run
entry.content:
If Official Document Numbering (ODN) has been activated, there may be Financial Accounting (FI) documents that were posted before ODN was introduced and therefore do not contain an ODN number. In addition, some FI-relevant posting processes may not currently be integrated with the standard ODN framework, such as postings originating from Flexible Real Estate Management (RE-FX) or other applications that create FI documents through interfaces not covered by ODN.

The ODN Insert for Accounting Documents report (transaction ODN_FI_INV_INSERT) addresses these scenarios by allowing an ODN value and legal timestamp to be added to FI documents that have already been posted. The report can be used for individual documents or for larger volumes through a mass upload process, regardless of the originating application or posting source.

Scope Note

Scope Note: This report applies to SAP S/4HANA (on-premise) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. It ships as part of the H1/2026 FI ODN framework enhancements together with the credit-memo / reversal / BAdI functionality. Availability starts at 2025 FPS02 and is back-ported to earlier releases via SAP Note 3739128. A prerequisite chain of seven additional SAP Notes must be applied in a specific order — see the Note Sequence section below.

Main Insight

The ODN Insert for Accounting Documents report closes a real operational gap. Whenever an FI document exists in the ledger without a matching entry in table GSLOG_LFD — or with an entry whose ODN value is empty — you now have a governed way to fix it without touching the accounting document itself.

Two things happen in one shot:

  1. The report writes (or overwrites an empty value) the ODN value on the accounting document.
  2. It writes (or overwrites an empty value) the ODN legal timestamp.

Two ways to run it:

  • Single-document mode — enter one document number, one company code, one fiscal year, one ODN Type, one ODN Value and one ODN Country; simulate; then commit.
  • Mass-upload mode — provide a CSV file with many document keys and the target ODN values. SAP Note 3739128 ships the file template.

The same report handles both customer and supplier invoice scenarios in Financial Accounting, and it applies whether the document was posted through FB01, FB70, FB60 (if ODN Value was left empty), FV70 + FBV0 (parked → posted), or any other FI entry point.

Two guardrails protect the process:

  • Users must be assigned to authorization object LFD_ODNREP to run Simulation and Update.
  • Only one ODN country per document — either the company-code country or the reporting country.

Where You'll See It in the System

1. The Selection Screen — Choose Your Target

The report entry screen lets you enter a single document (company code + document number + fiscal year) or upload a file for mass processing. You also select whether you want to Simulate or Update:

2. The Result — Traceable, Row by Row

After the run, every processed document is listed with its outcome — inserted, overwritten (only for empty records), or skipped with a reason. This log is the audit trail you'll hand to your compliance team:

3. Verification in FB03 — The ODN Is Now on the Document

Open the accounting document in FB03 and the ODN value now appears exactly where it would have if it had been captured at posting time (for system availability see SAP Note 3716857)

4. Additional Fiscal Data Tab — The Full Picture

On the Additional Fiscal Data tab of the same document, you see the full triple: ODN country, ODN value, and ODN legal timestamp — all three now maintained:

Two Typical Scenarios

Example 1 — Post-Enablement Backfill: You posted an FI invoicing document via transaction FB01 (Post Document) before ODN was enabled for that company code. The document has no ODN entry in GSLOG_LFD at all. Run the report in single-document or mass-upload mode to insert both the ODN value and the ODN legal timestamp — no reversal, no re-post.

Example 2 — Parked Document Cleanup: You created a parked customer invoice via transaction FV70 (Park Customer Invoice) and later approved it with FBV0 (Post Parked Document). Run the report against that single document to write the correct number and timestamp in place.

The BAdI Is Included

An easy misconception: "if the report writes directly to the tables, my custom numbering rules won't apply." Wrong. The ODN Insert report calls the BADI_FI_AD_ODN_MODIFY BAdI on every processed document — the same BAdI that fires during FB70 / FB75 / FB60 / FB65 entry, during FB08 reversals, and during BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST calls.

That means:

  • If you've implemented country-specific numbering logic in the BAdI, it runs automatically.

📖See the BAdI Help Portal page for the method signature and implementation guidance.

Note Sequence — Apply These in Order

If you're on a release below 2025 FPS02, implement the following SAP Notes step-by-step, in the exact sequence shown. Skipping the order can break dependencies between the prerequisite objects:

  1. 3708326 — FI ODN - Prerequisite Objects, BAdI (Manual Input Scenarios)
  2. 3729848 — FI ODN - Prerequisite Objects, Supplier Invoice Class III
  3. 3720120 — FI ODN - Prerequisite Objects, Customer Invoice Class III
  4. 3739126 — FI ODN - Prerequisite Objects, Update Service Class II
  5. 3739128 — FI ODN - ODN Insert for Accounting Documents (the report itself)
  6. 3776153 — FI ODN - Prerequisite Objects, Class for BTE Events
  7. 3742537 — FI ODN - BAdI in Accounting Transactions; BAPI and Reversal Enablers
  8. 3742475 — FI ODN - FI ODN functions available for transactions FB65 and FB75

Only after all eight notes are applied is the retrofit report fully wired to the rest of the FI ODN framework (BAdI, reversals, credit memos). If you're already on 2025 FPS02 or higher, the whole set is delivered with the release — no action needed.

Practical Takeaway

To adopt the ODN Insert report:

  • Confirm your release: 2025 FPS02 or newer, or an earlier release with the eight prerequisite SAP Notes applied in order
  • Run the customizing for your countries and processes in scope
  • Assign authorization object LFD_ODNREP to the users who will run Simulation and Update — and scope it to the ODN processes they're allowed to touch
  • Download the mass-upload file template attached to SAP Note 3739128
  • Run a Simulation first — on a single document, then on a representative mass-upload batch — and inspect the result list
  • Verify a handful of documents in FB03 (both the header and the Additional Fiscal Data tab) or via CDS view I_ACCOUNTINGDOCUMENTODN before scaling to production volumes
  • Coordinate the run with your compliance / audit team — the ODN legal timestamp is the value they'll ask about

Summary

The ODN Insert for Accounting Documents report (transaction ODN_FI_INV_INSERT) is the missing puzzle piece that lets you retrofit Official Document Numbers onto already-posted FI documents — either one at a time or thousands at once via mass upload (foreground processing: maximum 1,000 records; background processing: maximum 5,000 records). It calls the same BADI_FI_AD_ODN_MODIFY BAdI as every other FI ODN entry point, so your custom numbering logic applies automatically. Combined with the new FB08 reversal and FB65/FB75 credit-memo support, the FI ODN framework now covers a powerful scope.

Key Takeaways

  • Retrofit without reversal. Report ODN_FI_INV_INSERT writes ODN values and legal timestamps directly into already-posted FI documents — no reversal, no re-post, no modification of standard code.
  • Single or mass. Process one document interactively or upload a CSV file for bulk backfill; SAP Note 3739128 ships the file template.
  • The BAdI still fires. BADI_FI_AD_ODN_MODIFY is called during the retrofit run too — your country-specific numbering logic applies automatically, whether the document was posted yesterday or last year.
  • Authorization-controlled. Users need authorization object LFD_ODNREP to run Simulation and Update; scope it to the specific ODN processes they're allowed to touch.
  • One country per document. Either the company-code country or the reporting country.
  • Part of a bigger toolset. The retrofit report pairs with FB08 (reversals), FB65 (supplier credit memos), FB75 (customer credit memos), and the FI BAdI to give you complete FI ODN coverage.

Restrictions

  • The documents need to fit into the criteria to use one of the available ODN processes.
  • When the process is originated in logistics (cases of FI documents generated from sales or MM) this report inserts ODN only into the FI document.
  • Documents posted from modules for which there is not yet ODN support (such as Settlement Management) may fall back to FI ODN processes. It is advisable to use the report to cover these cases only if the ODN types can be customized for the FI processes consistently with the potential source processes.
  • In releases 103 and 102 there is currently no support for FB60 / FB65, FB70 / FB75, FB02 and FB03.
  • The reversal and BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST are enabled with simple ODN creation, meaning the ODN values are proposed initial, and the customer may adapt them using the BAdI.

More Information

  • Insertion of ODN in Accounting Documents — SAP Help Portal
  • BAdI: Modify ODN Value and Legal Timestamp for Scenarios in Financial Accounting (BADI_FI_AD_ODN_MODIFY) — SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Notes (in application order): 3708326, 3729848, 3720120, 3739126, 3739128, 3776153, 3742537, 3742475

entry.title: Re: Account 402101 requires an account assignment relevan...
entry.content:
The system is consistent:

If a G/L account is a primary cost element, it requires a cost object. The fact that the first GR worked means the account determination or valuation setup was different at that time and subsequently, it has been changed. The error now is because the last GR is hitting 402101 without a cost object.

Do not rely on OKB9 defaults unless that’s truly the business rule. Instead, correct the account determination (OBYC) or material/PO account assignment so that free stock postings hit a proper expense account (secondary cost element or non‑cost element G/L). If 402101 must be used, ensure a valid cost object is assigned via PO account assignment category.

entry.title: Re: Make Optional Check number field for F-36 transaction
entry.content:
Hi,

In S/4HANA, SAP has enhanced Bill of Exchange and post‑dated check processing to enforce stronger compliance and traceability, treating each bill or check as a uniquely identifiable financial instrument. 
As a result, the Check No. field is mandatory by standard design in S/4HANA and cannot be switched off or changed via configuration. 
This is an expected functional difference between ECC and S/4HANA and is not a defect.

For more information, kindly check below SAP Help portal:
Posting Incoming Checks | SAP Help Portal 

Best regards,
Monika
